headline: Round 5 substantively rebuilds the tray as a closed kanban bin with 4 real walls (back/sides at 30 mm, front wall short at 18 mm), an interior pan-edge floor ramp curving up to the front lip, and a grabbable exterior scoop on the +Y face. Cradle drops feet (silicone aftermarket) and cable notch (printer plug clears the 25 mm wall), and the top-edge fillet is rendered smooth at $fn=200 + 64-step stack.

dimensions.notes: Round-5: feet deleted (mesh now starts at z=0, not z=-3) and cable notch deleted (back wall continuous). Mesh Z-span = 25.001 (was 28.001 in round 4). $fn=200 and top_fillet_steps=64 produce a smooth top-edge curve with 64 slabs of ~0.047mm each — invisible at any reasonable render zoom and far below FDM layer height. Build volume margin Y = 1.1 mm (unchanged), X = 146 mm, Z = 231 mm.

Round-5 substantive rebuild. Tray ext_h grew from 21.6 → 30 mm (back/side walls). Front wall is short at 18 mm. Mesh y-extent = 96.7 = ext_d (94.2) + grab_boss_proud (2.5) — the grab boss extends 2.5 mm beyond the bin envelope on the +Y exterior. Top z = 29.952 (0.05 mm under nominal — the very top fillet roll's final inset reduces the topmost-corner z by a hair). Volume 76.14 cm³ is ~3.2x larger than round 4's 23.86 cm³ because of (a) taller bin (8.4 mm height bump), (b) substantial ramp solid mass at the front interior, (c) grab boss adding 2.6 cm³.

totals.change_breakdown: Cradle: +1.04 cm³ (no feet to subtract — round 4's feet were minor, top fillet stack adds tiny amount). Tray: +52.28 cm³ (taller + heavier ramp solid + grab boss).

fix1_tray_closed_kanban_bin_rebuild.description: Replaced round 4's curved-front-wall construction with a closed 4-wall bin. Back/side walls at full ext_h=30. Front wall short at 18mm (1.6mm thick, between side walls in central x range). Interior ramp: concave arc from (y=62.6, z=1.6) to (y=92.6, z=18), arc radius 22, centered on +Y/-Z side (the SHORT-arc bulging direction is +Z, into the cavity, giving a 'pan-edge' curve where slope is steepest at the back and shallowest at the front — matches the brief's functional intent of finger-slides-under-label-and-pushes-it-up-and-over). Exterior grab feature: a lip-thickening boss (66 × 16 × 2.5 mm proud, with rounded X-Z corners) on the +Y exterior, with a 50 × 14 × 2.86 mm concave capsule scoop carved into it at z=2..16. The scoop is centered horizontally and reads as 'grab here' with smooth fade-out at the X ends.
spec_diffs: DELETE: tray_scoop_top_recess, tray_scoop_arc_radius, tray_scoop_construction, tray_scoop_note (all from round 4)
ADD: ramp_y_extent=30, ramp_back_y=62.6, ramp_front_y=92.6, ramp_back_z=1.6, ramp_front_z=18, ramp_arc_radius=22, ramp_arc_construction (description)
ADD: grab_boss_proud=2.5, grab_boss_width=66, grab_boss_height_z=16, grab_boss_corner_r=5, grab_boss_construction (description)
ADD: grab_scoop_arc_r=10, grab_scoop_chord_z=14, grab_scoop_depth=2.86, grab_scoop_center_z=9, grab_scoop_width=50, grab_scoop_x_center=51.6, grab_scoop_construction (description)
ADD: tray_mesh_y_with_grab_boss=96.7 (extends past bin envelope by 2.5mm)
UPDATE: print_orientation_tray 'back face on bed' → 'face up (open top up), back on bed'

deviations.what: Front wall top edge is sharp (no r=2 fillet)
deviations.why: Wall_t=1.6 mm is too thin for an r=2 quarter-arc roll via slab-stack approach. A bullnose half-cylinder cap was attempted but produced a CGAL non-manifold artifact (disconnected mesh component) at the corner clipping. Decision: leave the front wall top as a sharp edge as a function-driven exception. Documented in spec.json under tray_front_wall_top_treatment_note.

deviations.what: Ramp arc center placed on +Y/-Z side (NOT +Y/+Z as modeler-notes-v5 stated)
deviations.why: Modeler-notes-v5 said 'r=22 places the arc center on the +Y/+Z exterior side, producing a concave curve' but the math doesn't work — a center on the +Y/+Z side at r=22 isn't equidistant from both endpoints. The two valid centers are at -Y/+Z (~70.96, 21.94) or +Y/-Z (~84.24, -2.34). The notes' written intent ('concave from inside, curves down-and-back') is ambiguous: literal interpretation gives the -Y/+Z center, which produces a curve that DIPS BELOW the chord (the floor first stays flat then rises sharply at the very front — not a useful ramp). The brief's functional description ('like sliding food out of a pan with a sloped front edge') describes the +Y/-Z center curve: a pan-edge profile where the floor rises smoothly at the back and plateaus toward the front lip. Chose +Y/-Z to match the functional intent. This curve is also fully self-supporting in face-up print orientation.

The notes specified a 2.86 mm-deep scoop on a 1.6 mm-thick front wall — that geometry would punch through the wall, leaving a hole instead of an indent. Added a 2.5 mm-proud lip-thickening boss (66 × 16 mm with rounded corners) so the local wall thickness becomes 4.1 mm, which supports the scoop with 1.24 mm of wall remaining at the deepest point. Documented as an intrinsic functional element rather than decoration.

deviations.what: Tray mesh y-extent 96.7 mm (boss extends past ext_d=94.2)
deviations.why: Boss adds 2.5 mm of material proud of the front wall +Y face. Mesh bbox includes the boss; bin envelope ext_d=94.2 still applies for the slot fit. Echoed dimension reports 96.7 (mesh-actual).

fix2_cradle_delete_feet.spec_diff: DELETE corner_feet() module from cradle.scad. DELETE foot_d, foot_h, foot_inset, foot_blend_r params. DELETE fillet_foot_to_plate_r param. UPDATE cradle_total_h: 28 → 25 (no feet below datum). UPDATE dimensions.z and echoedDimensions.cradle.z: 28 → 25.
fix2_cradle_delete_feet.result: Mesh Z-span goes from 28.001 (round-4 with feet at z=-3..25) to 25.001 (round-5 flush-base). Base plate's full footprint sits on the bed (much better first-layer adhesion than 4 small feet circles). User will apply silicone feet aftermarket.

fix3_cradle_delete_cable_notch.spec_diff: DELETE cable_slot_cutter() module from cradle.scad. DELETE cable_slot_w, cable_slot_h, cable_slot_z_bottom, cable_slot_z_top, cable_slot_center_x_from_left_exterior, cable_slot_style params. DROP the cable_slot_h < low_wall_h and low_wall_h - cable_slot_h >= 4 asserts.
fix3_cradle_delete_cable_notch.result: Back wall is a clean continuous 25 mm band. Cable runs over the top of the back wall (the printer's USB+DC connectors sit above the 25 mm wall height, so no notch is needed).

fix4_cradle_smooth_top_fillet.spec_diff: BUMP cradle $fn from 80 → 200. BUMP cradle slab-stack steps from 8 → 64. Add new spec params cradle_fn=200, cradle_top_fillet_steps=64.
fix4_cradle_smooth_top_fillet.result: Each fillet slab is now r/64 ≈ 0.047 mm tall — well below FDM layer height (0.2 mm) and below visual perception. The cradle's top-edge fillet reads as a true continuous quarter-circle in renders, no stair-stepping at any zoom. (Render time bumped from ~2 min to ~15 min, which is acceptable for the smoothness gain.)

fix5_render_set_regenerated.spec_diff: No spec change. Same 7 views, regenerated for round-5 geometry.
fix5_render_set_regenerated.result: All 7 renders captured: cradle-user-front-in-use, cradle-user-front-threequarter-in-use, cradle-user-front, cradle-user-front-threequarter, cradle-user-top-threequarter, tray-user-front, tray-user-front-threequarter. Direct openscad invocation with explicit eye+center cameras (cli-anything-openscad presets remain unusable per all prior rounds' lessons). The headline test render (tray-user-front-threequarter) shows: closed 4-wall bin ✓, interior ramp visible as smooth concave curve ✓, grabbable +Y scoop centered + visible ✓.

specDeviations.reason: Fix 2: feet deleted. Mesh extends z=0..25 (was z=-3..25).
Fix 1: ext_h bumped 8.4 mm to provide room for the interior ramp + lip + grab feature. Tray sticks ~5 mm proud of the 25 mm cradle wall — intentional per brief.
Fix 1: grab boss adds 2.5 mm of material proud of the front wall +Y face. Bin envelope (ext_d) still 94.2 — the boss is the sculptural lip-thickening hosting the scoop.
Round-4 carry-over (slot side walls 3.05 mm matching wall_thickness).

specDeviations.round_4_value: back face on bed (-Y down), open top facing +Y in print-frame
specDeviations.round_5_value: face up (open top up), back on bed
specDeviations.reason: Round 5 reverts to face-up because the new pan-edge floor ramp is fully self-supporting (no overhang in face-up orientation). Front wall is short and structurally fine. Grab scoop indent is shallow enough to print without supports.

features.name: base_plate
features.description: Stepped base plate with r=10 hero corner radius. Footprint follows stepped_footprint_raw — narrow under printer section (X=12..98, Y=0..160), wider under shelf (X=0..110, Y=160..254.9). Concave r=10 quarter-arc on both ±X sides at the printer→shelf transition.

features.name: low_wall_block
features.description: 25 mm tall stepped perimeter wall block. All 4 perimeter walls uniform 25 mm. Wall thickness 3 mm uniform on the printer-section walls; tray-slot side walls 3.05 mm. Top edge r=3 utility fillet rolled via 64-step quarter-arc stack at $fn=200 (round-5 smoother than round 4's 8-step stack at $fn=80). Exterior vertical corner edges r=10 hero. Carved interior provides the printer pocket (80 × 154 × 25 mm visible) and the tray slot opening (open-top + open-front, 103.9 × 94.9 × 22.3 mm). NO cable notch in round 5.

features.name: printer_shelf_concave_fillet
features.description: r=10 concave quarter-arc on both ±X sides where the 86 mm printer section transitions to the 110 mm shelf section. The one sculptural move on the cradle proper. Self-supporting (concave with material on the inside of the curve, no overhang).

features.name: host_object_proxy
features.description: RENDER-ONLY printer proxy box (78 × 152 × 143 mm) at the installed position inside the printer pocket. Gated by render_with_host parameter — default false.

featuresTray.name: tray_floor
featuresTray.description: Solid floor at z = 0..floor_t (0..1.6 mm) at the back portion of the bin (y = wall_t..ramp_back_y = 1.6..62.6). Full interior width.

featuresTray.name: tray_walls_back_sides
featuresTray.description: Back wall (-Y) and ±X side walls — vertical, rounded_rect outer outline with r=3 vertical edge fillet, uniform 1.6 mm thickness. Top edges (z = ext_h = 30) carry a continuous r=2 quarter-arc fillet rolled inward via 64-step slab stack at $fn=200.

featuresTray.name: tray_front_wall
featuresTray.description: Short front wall (+Y direction). Material at y = ext_d-wall_t..ext_d (92.6..94.2), x = wall_t..ext_w-wall_t (1.6..101.6, between side walls), z = 0..18. Top edge SHARP (no r=2 fillet — function-driven exception per spec, since wall_t=1.6 is too thin for an r=2 quarter-arc roll).

featuresTray.name: interior_floor_ramp
featuresTray.description: Concave-arc floor surface near the front of the bin. Connects (y=62.6, z=1.6) (back end of ramp at flat-floor level) to (y=92.6, z=18) (front end at top of front wall). Arc center on +Y/-Z side at C ≈ (84.24, -2.34); arc bulges UP into the cavity (slope steepest at back, shallowest at front — pan-edge curve). Self-supporting in face-up print orientation. Full interior X width (x = wall_t..ext_w-wall_t = 1.6..101.6).

featuresTray.name: grab_boss
featuresTray.description: Lip-thickening on +Y exterior of the front wall. 66 × 16 × 2.5 mm (W × H × proud), rounded X-Z corners r=5. Built as the hull of 4 Y-axis cylinders. Adds local wall thickness (1.6 + 2.5 = 4.1 mm) sufficient to support the 2.86 mm-deep scoop.

featuresTray.name: grab_scoop
featuresTray.description: Concave horizontal indent on the +Y exterior face of the grab_boss. 50 × 14 × 2.86 mm (visible W × H × deepest depth), centered at (x=51.6, z=9). Built as the hull of two r=10 spheres centered at (x=33.6, y=103.84, z=9) and (x=69.6, y=103.84, z=9). On the boss outer face (y=96.7), the cutter's intersection is an oval indent fading from full chord at x=33.6..69.6 down to a single point at x=26.6 and x=76.6. Reads as 'grab here' with smooth fade-out at the ends.

cradle.bed_face: base plate bottom (z = 0 — flush with bed, NO feet in round 5)
cradle.notes: Round-5 simplifies adhesion: no detached feet. Base plate's full footprint sits on the bed. No overhang exceeds 45° at any visible feature. The 5 mm bridge above cable notch (round 4) is GONE in round 5 (no notch).

tray.bed_face: back face (-Y on bed)? OR floor (z=0 on bed)? — round 5 reverts to FLOOR ON BED (face up, open top up).
tray.notes: Round-5 print orientation FLIP back from round 4: face-up. The pan-edge interior ramp is fully self-supporting (the surface tangent always points up-and-forward, never overhanging). The grab scoop's concave indent is shallow enough (2.86 mm depth, 14 mm chord at r=10) that local tangent angles stay within 45°-from-vertical envelope — printable without supports. Verify in slicer; no supports expected. The grab boss bottom face (z=0, y=ext_d..ext_d+2.5) prints as a 66 × 2.5 mm first-layer island — 165 mm² adhesion area, fine for PLA on textured PEI.

userOrientationConfirmed.user_front: +Y face (tray slides in/out here; printer faces user; grab scoop on tray exterior)
userOrientationConfirmed.user_back: -Y face (against-wall, no notch — cable runs over top of wall)
seams_hidden_on: tray's exterior corners away from grab feature

render_method: Direct openscad invocation with explicit eye+center cameras (--camera=eye_x,eye_y,eye_z,center_x,center_y,center_z form). cli-anything-openscad presets remain unusable per prior rounds' lessons. Combined-cradle-tray harness files (.combined-use.scad, .combined-bare.scad in design dir) used for views that show both parts in the same frame. Tray-only views use tray.scad directly.

tray_closed_kanban_bin.details: tray-user-front-threequarter shows clearly the 4 walls (back, ±sides at full height; front short with grab boss). The bin reads as a closed kanban container, not an open bowl. Closed-bin character restored from round 4's curved-front-wall mistake.

tray_interior_ramp_curve.details: Interior ramp visible as a smooth concave curve from flat-floor (back) up to the top of the front wall. Pan-edge curve (slope steepest at back, plateaus at front) matches the brief's 'sliding food out of a pan' analogy. No kinks, no inflection points.

tray_grabbable_scoop.details: tray-user-front shows a centered oval indent on the front face — clearly readable as 'grab here'. Smooth fade-out at left/right ends (capsule end-caps). The boss/scoop combination is more sculptural than the round-4 recessed lip — much more obvious as a hand-engagement feature.

cradle_smooth_top_edge.details: $fn=200 + 64-step fillet stack produces slabs ~0.047 mm tall. No stair-stepping visible at any render zoom. Fillet reads as a true continuous quarter-circle.

cradle_flush_base_no_notch.details: Mesh Z-span 25.001 mm = low_wall_h. No feet protruding below z=0. Back wall is continuous (no cable notch). cradle-user-top-threequarter confirms clean back wall band; cradle-user-front-in-use confirms flush base.

uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.front_wall_h_18mm: Reads cleanly relative to 30 mm back wall — clear hierarchy without feeling cramped. The asymmetry IS the bin's character.
uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.ramp_y_extent_30mm: Ramp occupies the front 30 mm of the 91 mm interior depth — leaves 60 mm of flat back floor for labels to land. Proportions feel right.
uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.ramp_arc_radius_22mm: r=22 produces a ramp where the curve is most pronounced at the back third (steep rise) and plateaus over the front third. Functionally good; visually confirmed in tray-user-front-threequarter render.
uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.grab_scoop_dimensions_50x14x2.86: Dimensions feel right — clearly reads as 'grab me' but doesn't dominate the 18 mm front wall. The boss makes it work structurally despite the 1.6 mm wall.
uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.tray_height_30mm_vs_cradle_25mm: 5mm overhang reads as 'lip-grab intentional' in cradle-user-front-in-use. Visually distinguishable but not jarring.
uncertainItemsFromV5Notes.print_orientation_face_up: Confirmed self-supporting. Pan-edge ramp surface always angles up-and-forward, never overhangs. Verify in slicer pass.
